repo_prior_art.cite: none; doc/API.md PartialID catalogue has only Manski nonparametric bound wrappers

published_prior_art.cite: FrangakisRubin2002 and GilbertHudgens2008: principal-stratum endpoint effects; decomposition is law of total expectation
Kuroki2013 Stat Med: closed-form sharp bounds using a surrogate endpoint; YinLiuGengLuo2019: optimal observed-data criteria excluding surrogate paradox
MaYinLiuGeng2021: individual surrogate paradox and sharp criteria; YinLiuGengLuo2019: optimal criteria for surrogate paradox exclusion

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: KurokiCai2013 metadata appears unstable/incorrect: the cited Biometrics 69(4), 951--960 paper was not verified; the closest verified paper is Kuroki 2013 Stat Med, and MaYinLiuGeng2021 is Biostatistics 22(1), 97--113 rather than 22(2), 312--327.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The average-margin decomposition is routine principal-strata total expectation, not a novel theorem beyond FrangakisRubin2002/GilbertHudgens2008.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The closest sharp-bounds comparator is mis-entered: Kuroki 2013 Stat Med directly derives closed-form sharp bounds using a surrogate endpoint and must be compared location-specifically.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The advertised constructive object W(P,eta) includes L_R^{obs}(P), but Exhibit 9.1 does not compute observed arm tables or L_R^{obs}(P) from P.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The phase threshold tau*(P,eta) is not exhibited from a full finite observed law; the example computes only (eta-nu-a)/r and omits the observed-feasibility term.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Because P is the randomized observed law of (Z,S,Y), Delta_Y is already identified by arm means; the constraint Delta_Y>=eta is redundant when Delta_Y(P)>=eta and infeasible otherwise, so W(P,eta) is missing the observed Delta_Y(P) residual.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The claimed phase transition in eta(t) cannot hold with fixed observed P: varying eta below the identified Delta_Y(P) does not change the feasible latent set, and varying it above Delta_Y(P) makes the set empty.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1 / Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: tau* is defined inconsistently across the locked block, the Introduction result list, and Conjecture 2: sometimes as (eta-nu-a)/r and sometimes as max{L_R^{obs}(P),(eta-nu-a)/r}.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: After fixing the Delta_Y(P) issue, the proof route is a routine one-dimensional support/intersection calculation, below the flagship threshold.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Redefine the observable regime so the endpoint margin is genuinely external/unobserved, or replace eta by the identified Delta_Y(P) and derive the true sharp responder bound from the four arm-specific (S,Y) mixture equations.
Add a full finite-table exhibit: specify P(Z=z,S=s,Y=y), recover (nu,r,a), compute L_R^{obs}(P), compute W(P,eta), and verify the active face is feasible.

Fix the literature checklist and bibliography: replace KurokiCai2013 with the verified sharp-bounds comparator, correct MaYinLiuGeng2021 metadata, and add section/equation anchors for the closest prior results.

recommended_next_step: Pivot or substantially redraft: the current eta-threshold kernel is invalid because Delta_Y is already identified from the stated observed law.
